claim: ACP (Agent Client Protocol) is a JSON RPC standard for connecting any coding agent to any code editor, similar to MCP but for AI development tools
actionable: Support ACP in your coding assistant or IDE; use existing TypeScript examples in the Zed repo to build adapters for multiple agents in ~600 lines
source_title: This New Protocol Will Change AI Coding Forever (ACP)

claim: Thread history in Zed's ACP integration is non-functional in beta; this limits continuity for multi-turn code generation tasks
actionable: When using ACP in Zed beta, manually maintain context across threads or expect to repeat context from earlier sessions
source_title: This New Protocol Will Change AI Coding Forever (ACP)

claim: Building a custom ACP agent requires only an LLM, tool use, and JSON serialization; the protocol abstracts away IDE specifics
actionable: Build custom coding agents by implementing session management, tool calls, and ACP JSON serialization; you gain IDE compatibility across Zed, VS Code, and others
source_title: This New Protocol Will Change AI Coding Forever (ACP)

claim: Claude Code adapter for Zed converts SDK messages into ACP format; only ~600 lines of data transformation needed despite SDK complexity
actionable: Create agent adapters by mapping your agent's output format to ACP JSON; focus on type transformations and metadata preservation
source_title: This New Protocol Will Change AI Coding Forever (ACP)
